title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI on AWS and Slackbot's AI Upgrade
summary: OpenAI models hit AWS, Slackbot gets an AI boost, and the workplace AI battle heats up.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Let's dive into the latest news. OpenAI's frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS. This move gives enterprises a new way to tap into OpenAI's tech without leaving their AWS environments. It's like having your favorite AI tools at your fingertips, right where you're already working.

OpenAI's models are now generally available on AWS. This is a big deal because it lets companies use OpenAI's powerful tools within the AWS ecosystem they're already familiar with. No need to switch contexts or learn new workflows. It's a smooth integration, which is great news for those who want to leverage AI without the hassle.

Salesforce has revamped Slackbot, its workplace assistant, turning it into a full-fledged AI agent. This upgrade is part of Salesforce's push to stay competitive in the workplace AI space, where Microsoft and Google are also playing. The new Slackbot can handle more complex tasks, making it a more useful tool for teams.

What's interesting here is how these moves reflect the evolving AI landscape. We're seeing more platform shifts, with major players like AWS and Salesforce integrating AI into their offerings. This isn't just about adding new features; it's about changing how businesses operate. The economics of AI are also in play, as companies look for ways to make AI more accessible and affordable.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
